task_completion.description: Access the Floreant POS Back Office using the manager PIN.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Floreant POS Back Office dashboard or main menu is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Open the discount or coupon creation dialog.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A window or dialog specifically for adding or creating a new discount/coupon is open.
The text field for the coupon or discount name contains exactly 'Weekend Special'.
The text field for the discount value or amount contains '20' or '20.0'.

task_completion.description: Set the discount type to a percentage-based discount.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A UI element (such as a dropdown, radio button, or checkbox) indicating a percentage discount (e.g., '%', 'Percentage Per Item', 'Percentage Per Order') is selected.

task_completion.description: Save the coupon and verify it appears in the discount list.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main discount/coupon list or data table is visible and contains a saved entry for 'Weekend Special' showing a value of 20.

integrity.description: The agent used the Floreant POS graphical user interface to create the coupon.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Floreant POS application windows being interacted with, rather than terminal commands, SQL clients, or scripts.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the UI to manipulate the database directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is a logical progression of UI interactions (opening the dialog, filling out fields, and clicking save) rather than the coupon magically appearing in the list.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Floreant POS software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The standard Floreant POS Java-based UI is visible, not a text editor, web browser, or mock application.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected Coupon Name: 'Weekend Special'. Expected Value: 20.0. Manager PIN for Back Office access: '1111'. Note: The VLM should look for UI elements indicating a percentage discount (e.g., '%' symbol or 'Percentage' options) in the GUI.
